description: Utilities for with-statement contexts. Provides decorators and helpers for creating and working with context managers.

functions.name: contextmanager

params.name: func
params.description: A generator function that yields exactly once. Code before the yield runs on __enter__, code after runs on __exit__.

returns.description: A decorator that converts the generator function into a context manager factory

functions.description: Decorator that converts a generator function into a context manager. The function must yield exactly once. Everything before the yield is executed on entering the context; everything after is executed on leaving. The yielded value is bound to the 'as' target.

functions.name: nested

params.name: *managers
params.description: One or more context manager objects to nest

returns.description: A combined context manager that enters all supplied managers in order

functions.description: Combines multiple context managers into a single nested context manager. Equivalent to nesting multiple with statements. Deprecated since Python 2.7 in favor of the multiple-with syntax: with A() as a, B() as b.

classes.name: closing
classes.description: A context manager that ensures the wrapped object's close() method is called when the with block exits. Useful for objects like urllib.urlopen() results that have close() but are not context managers.

params.name: thing
params.description: Any object with a close() method

constructors.description: Wrap an object so its close() method is called on context exit.

methods.name: close
methods.description: Calls the wrapped object's close() method. Invoked automatically when exiting the with block.
